summary: A strong, well-targeted SRE resume with truthful metrics and excellent JD alignment; only minor consistency and labeling issues remain.

issues.issue: The Salon project tech line lists 'CloudFormation' but the bullet references 'SAM/CloudFormation templates' — the SAM tool is dropped from the tech stack header while still cited in the bullet. The master brief tech line for this project explicitly includes 'SAM'.
issues.where: Salon project tech: "AWS Lambda · CloudFormation · API Gateway · RDS PostgreSQL · Amazon EFS · Secrets Manager · Python" vs bullet "6 nested SAM/CloudFormation templates"
issues.suggestion: Add SAM back to the tech line: 'AWS Lambda · SAM · CloudFormation · API Gateway ...' to match the bullet and the source brief.

issues.issue: Role title 'DevOps Engineer (Site Reliability Engineer)' invents a parenthetical SRE title the candidate never held. The real title was 'DevOps Engineer' at eSparkBiz. Re-titling risks an honesty/verification flag.
issues.suggestion: Use the actual title 'DevOps Engineer'. SRE framing is fine in the summary/bullets, but do not append a title the candidate did not hold.

issues.issue: Istio appears in the master Tier-2 stack but is intentionally omitted here (acceptable per JD-driven trimming). However ELK Stack and Datadog were dropped from observability while the master B4 bullet includes them — the resume's observability bullet correctly omits them, which is fine, but ensure no orphan reference remains. No orphan found; flagging only that the trimmed stack is consistent.
issues.where: Skills 'Observability & Monitoring' row vs master B4 bullet
issues.suggestion: No change needed — trimming is consistent. Confirm intentional.

issues.issue: Intern dates use 'May 2025' (full month) while other date formats are consistent; the full-time end is 'Apr 2026' (abbreviated). Both abbreviated forms are fine, but 'May' is not abbreviated whereas 'Jun', 'Jan', 'Apr' are — May is naturally 3 letters so this is acceptable, just noting for parallelism.
issues.suggestion: No change required; consistent abbreviation already used.

issues.issue: JD is for 'Site Reliability Engineer - I'. Resume surfaces strong SRE keywords (SLI/SLO, MTTR, incident response, post-mortems, runbooks, on-call). Coverage is good. Consider adding 'on-call' explicitly as a skill keyword if the JD emphasizes rotations, though it appears in bullets.
issues.where: Summary and Reliability & Incident Response skills row
issues.suggestion: Optionally add 'On-call readiness' to the Reliability row for exact ATS keyword match if JD lists on-call.

issues.issue: Healthcare prototype bullets correctly avoid the unverified production-scale metrics ('500+ users', '5+ IAM roles removed', '30% latency reduction') per the brief's warning — this is correct and compliant. No fabrication detected.
issues.suggestion: No change — properly kept at prototype scale.

issues.issue: Contact info (phone, email, LinkedIn, GitHub, location) is not visible in this JSON payload but is present in cv.md header; assuming the renderer injects it. If the renderer relies solely on this JSON, contact block is missing.
issues.where: Generated resume JSON has no contact/header fields
issues.suggestion: Verify the HTML renderer pulls contact info from profile.yml/cv.md so name, phone, email, LinkedIn, GitHub, and location appear in the header.
